statements.name: PROC SUPERLEARNER
statements.help: PROC SUPERLEARNER <options>; BASELEARNER 'name' model-type<(model-options)><input-options>; CROSSVALIDATION <options>; INPUT variables </option>; MARGIN 'name' variable='value' <...variable='value'>; OUTPUT OUT=libref.data-table<options>; STORE <OUT=>libref.data-table; TARGET variable </option>;

arguments.name: APPLYROWORDER
arguments.description: uses group and order information from the input data table

arguments.name: DATA=
arguments.description: names the input data table for PROC SUPERLEARNER to use

arguments.name: METHOD=
arguments.description: specifies the meta-learning method to be used to estimate the coefficients of the super learner model

arguments.name: RESTORE=
arguments.description: specifies the name of the item store that contains a model that is fitted and stored from a previous analysis

arguments.name: SEED=
arguments.description: specifies the initial seed to start the pseudorandom number generator for k-fold partitioning and base learner model building, where n is an integer

statements.name: BASELEARNER
statements.description: provides options for you to specify and customize a base learner model. You must first specify a unique name for the base learner in single quotes

arguments.name: BART
arguments.description: specifies a Bayesian additive regression trees model

arguments.name: MINLEAFSIZE=
arguments.description: specifies the minimum number of observations that each child of a split must contain in the training data in order for the split to be considered

arguments.name: NBI=
arguments.description: specifies the number of burn-in iterations to perform before the procedure starts to save samples for prediction

arguments.name: NBINS=
arguments.description: specifies the number of bins to use for binning the continuous input variables

arguments.name: NMC=
arguments.description: specifies the number of iterations in the main simulation loop

arguments.name: NTHIN=
arguments.description: specifies the thinning rate of the simulation

arguments.name: NTREE=
arguments.description: specifies the number of trees in a sample of the sum-of-trees ensemble

arguments.name: INTINPUT=
arguments.description: specifies the interval input variables for the base learner model

arguments.name: NOMINPUT=
arguments.description: specifies the nominal input variables for the base learner model

arguments.name: BNET
arguments.description: specifies a Bayesian network model

arguments.name: ALPHA=
arguments.description: specifies the significance level for independence tests by using the chi-square or G-square statistics, where number is between 0 and 1, inclusive

arguments.name: INDEPTEST=
arguments.description: specifies the method to use for independence tests

arguments.name: MAXPARENTS=
arguments.description: specifies the maximum number of parents that are allowed for each node in the network structure, where integer is a value between 1 and 16, inclusive

arguments.name: MIALPHA=
arguments.description: specifies the threshold for independence tests by using mutual information, where number is a value between 0 and 1, inclusive

arguments.name: NUMBIN=
arguments.description: specifies the number of binning levels for all interval variables, where integer is a value between 2 and 1,024, inclusive

arguments.name: PARENTING=
arguments.description: specifies the algorithm to use for orienting the network structure

arguments.name: PRESCREENING=
arguments.description: specifies the initial screening for the input variables

arguments.name: STRUCTURE=
arguments.description: specifies the network structure

arguments.name: VARSELECT=
arguments.description: specifies how input variables are selected beyond the prescreening

arguments.name: INTINPUT=
arguments.description: specifies the interval input variables for the base learner model

arguments.name: NOMINPUT=
arguments.description: specifies the nominal input variables for the base learner model

arguments.name: FACTMAC
arguments.description: specifies a factorization machine model

arguments.name: LEARNSTEP=
arguments.description: specifies the learning step size for the stochastic gradient descent algorithm, where number is a positive real number

arguments.name: MAXITER=
arguments.description: specifies the maximum number of iterations for the algorithm to perform, where number is an integer greater than or equal to 1

arguments.name: NFACTORS=
arguments.description: specifies the number of factors to estimate for the model, where number is an integer greater than or equal to 1

arguments.name: INTINPUT=
arguments.description: specifies the interval input variables for the base learner model

arguments.name: NOMINPUT=
arguments.description: specifies the nominal input variables for the base learner model

arguments.name: FOREST
arguments.description: specifies a forest model

arguments.name: INBAGFRACTION=
arguments.description: specifies the fraction of the random bootstrap sample of the training data to be used to grow each tree in the forest, where number is a value between 0 and 1, inclusive

arguments.name: MAXDEPTH=
arguments.description: specifies the maximum depth of the tree to be grown

arguments.name: MINLEAFSIZE=
arguments.description: specifies the minimum number of observations that each child of a split must contain in the training data table in order for the split to be considered

arguments.name: NTREES=
arguments.description: specifies the number of trees to grow in the forest model

arguments.name: NUMBIN=
arguments.description: specifies the number of bins to use for binning the interval input variables

arguments.name: VARS_TO_TRY=
arguments.description: specifies the number of input variables to consider splitting on in a node, where m ranges from 1 to the number of input variables

arguments.name: INTINPUT=
arguments.description: specifies the interval input variables for the base learner model

arguments.name: NOMINPUT=
arguments.description: specifies the nominal input variables for the base learner model

arguments.name: GAMMOD
arguments.description: specifies a generalized additive model based on low-rank regression splines

arguments.name: CLASS=
arguments.description: names the classification variables to be used as explanatory variables in the analysis if they are also used in the PARAM(effects) option

arguments.name: PARAM
arguments.description: specifies parametric effects that are constructed from variables in the input data; this option can appear multiple times

arguments.name: SPLINE
arguments.description: specifies nonparametric spline effects that are constructed from variables in the input data

arguments.name: DF=
arguments.description: specifies a fixed degrees of freedom

arguments.name: M=
arguments.description: specifies the order of the derivative in the penalty term, where n is a positive integer

arguments.name: GAMSELECT
arguments.description: specifies a generalized additive model with model selection

arguments.name: PARTBYFRAC
arguments.description: randomly assigns specified proportions of the observations in the input data table to the validation role

arguments.name: PARTBYVAR=
arguments.description: names the variable in the input data table whose values are used to assign the validation role to each observation

arguments.name: SELECTION=
arguments.description: specifies the method to be used to select the model

arguments.name: CHOOSE=
arguments.description: specifies the criterion to use in selecting the final model

arguments.name: MAXITER=
arguments.description: specifies the maximum number of iterations for the boosting method

arguments.name: STEPSIZE=
arguments.description: specifies the step size to use for the boosting algorithm, where number is a value between 0 and 1

arguments.name: LAMBDA1=
arguments.description: sets a fixed nonnegative value to control the sparsity penalty

arguments.name: LAMBDA2=
arguments.description: sets a fixed nonnegative value to control the smoothness penalty

arguments.name: MAXITER=
arguments.description: specifies the maximum number of iterations that generalized additive model fitting can perform by solving reweighted additive models at each iteration

arguments.name: CLASS=
arguments.description: names the classification variables to be used as explanatory variables in the analysis if they are also used in the PARAM(effects) option

arguments.name: PARAM
arguments.description: specifies parametric effects that are constructed from variables in the input data; you can specify this option multiple times

arguments.name: SPLINE
arguments.description: specifies nonparametric spline effects that are constructed from variables in the input data

arguments.name: DEGREE=
arguments.description: specifies the degree of the spline transformation, where n is a nonnegative integer

arguments.name: DF=
arguments.description: specifies the fixed degrees of freedom for the spline at each boosting iteration

arguments.name: GPCLASS
arguments.description: specifies a Gaussian process classification model

arguments.name: KERNEL=
arguments.description: specifies the kernel-related parameters to use in the Gaussian process

arguments.name: CONSTANT=
arguments.description: specifies the constant in the linear kernel function

arguments.name: SIGMA=
arguments.description: specifies the bandwidth in the Gaussian kernel function

arguments.name: MAXITER=
arguments.description: specifies the maximum number of Newton iterations

arguments.name: MINLEAFSIZE=
arguments.description: specifies the minimum change of the loss function between consecutive Newton iterations

arguments.name: INTINPUT=
arguments.description: specifies the interval input variables for the base learner model

arguments.name: GPREG
arguments.description: specifies a Gaussian process regression model

arguments.name: ALGORITHM=
arguments.description: specifies the optimization-algorithm to use during training

arguments.name: AUTORELEVANCEDETERMINATION
arguments.description: uses automatic relevance determination in the kernel function

arguments.name: JITTERMAXITERS=
arguments.description: specifies the maximum number of iterations for jitter Cholesky decomposition

arguments.name: KERNEL=
arguments.description: specifies the kernel-related parameters to be used in the Gaussian process

arguments.name: LEARNINGRATE=
arguments.description: specifies the learning rate parameter for the adam or SGD algorithm

arguments.name: MINIBATCHSIZE=
arguments.description: specifies the size of the minibatches to use in the adam or SGD algorithm

arguments.name: MOMENTUM=
arguments.description: specifies the momentum, where number is a value between 0 and 1, inclusive

arguments.name: NINDUCINGPOINTS=
arguments.description: specifies the number of inducing points to use in the sparse Gaussian process, where number is a positive integer

arguments.name: INTINPUT=
arguments.description: specifies the interval input variables for the base learner model

arguments.name: GRADBOOST
arguments.description: specifies a gradient boosting model

arguments.name: LASSO=
arguments.description: specifies the L1 norm regularization parameter, where number must be nonnegative

arguments.name: LEARNINGRATE=
arguments.description: specifies the learning rate for the gradient boosting algorithm, where number is a value between 0 and 1, inclusive

arguments.name: MAXDEPTH=
arguments.description: specifies the maximum depth of the tree to be grown

arguments.name: MINLEAFSIZE=
arguments.description: specifies the minimum number of observations that each child of a split must contain in the training data table in order for the split to be considered

arguments.name: NTREES=
arguments.description: specifies the number of trees to grow in the gradient boosting model

arguments.name: NUMBIN=
arguments.description: specifies the number of bins to use for binning the interval input variables

arguments.name: RIDGE=
arguments.description: specifies the L2 norm regularization parameter, where number must be nonnegative

arguments.name: SAMPLINGRATE=
arguments.description: specifies the fraction of the training data to be used to grow each tree in the boosting model

arguments.name: VARS_TO_TRY=
arguments.description: specifies the number of input variables to consider splitting on in a node, where m ranges from 1 to the number of input variables

arguments.name: INTINPUT=
arguments.description: specifies the interval input variables for the base learner model

arguments.name: NOMINPUT=
arguments.description: specifies the nominal input variables for the base learner model

arguments.name: LIGHTGRADBOOST
arguments.description: specifies a light gradient boosting machine model

arguments.name: BAGGINGFRACTION=
arguments.description: randomly selects a portion of the observations without resampling

arguments.name: BAGGINGFREQUENCY=
arguments.description: specifies the frequency of bagging

arguments.name: INPUTFRACTION=
arguments.description: randomly selects a subset of features on each iteration or tree

arguments.name: LASSO=
arguments.description: specifies the L1-norm regularization parameter, where number must be nonnegative

arguments.name: LEAFSIZE=
arguments.description: specifies the minimum number of observations that each child of a split must contain in the training data table in order for the split to be considered

arguments.name: LEARNINGRATE=
arguments.description: specifies the learning rate for each tree, where number must be greater than 0

arguments.name: MAXDEPTH=
arguments.description: limits the maximum depth of the tree model

arguments.name: MAXITERS=
arguments.description: specifies the maximum number of iterations for the boosting

arguments.name: NUMBIN=
arguments.description: specifies the number of bins to use for binning the interval input variables

arguments.name: RIDGE=
arguments.description: specifies the L2-norm regularization parameter, where number must be nonnegative

arguments.name: INTINPUT=
arguments.description: specifies the interval input variables for the base learner model

arguments.name: NOMINPUT=
arguments.description: specifies the nominal input variables for the base learner model

arguments.name: LOGSELECT
arguments.description: specifies a logistic regression model with model selection

arguments.name: LASSORHO=
arguments.description: specifies the base regularization parameter for the LASSO model selection method

arguments.name: LASSOSTEPS=
arguments.description: specifies the maximum number of steps for LASSO model selection

arguments.name: LASSOTOL=
arguments.description: specifies the convergence tolerance for the optimization algorithm that solves for the LASSO parameter estimates at each step of LASSO model selection

arguments.name: LINK=
arguments.description: specifies the link function for the model

arguments.name: PARTBYFRAC
arguments.description: randomly assigns specified proportions of the observations in the input data table to the validation role

arguments.name: PARTBYVAR=
arguments.description: names the variable in the input data table whose values are used to assign the validation role to each observation

arguments.name: SELECTION=
arguments.description: specifies the method to be used to select the model

arguments.name: CLASS=
arguments.description: names the classification variables to be used as explanatory variables in the analysis if you also specify them in the EFFECT= option

arguments.name: EFFECT=
arguments.description: specifies model effects that are constructed from variables in the input data table

arguments.name: REGSELECT
arguments.description: specifies an ordinary least squares regression model with model selection

arguments.name: PARTBYFRAC
arguments.description: randomly assigns specified proportions of the observations in the input data table to the validation role

arguments.name: PARTBYVAR=
arguments.description: names the variable in the input data table whose values are used to assign the validation role to each observation

arguments.name: SELECTION=
arguments.description: specifies the method to be used to select the model

arguments.name: CLASS=
arguments.description: names the classification variables to be used as explanatory variables in the analysis if you also specify them in the EFFECT= option

arguments.name: EFFECT=
arguments.description: specifies model effects that are constructed from variables in the input data table

arguments.name: SVMACHINE
arguments.description: specifies a support vector machine model

arguments.name: C=
arguments.description: specifies the penalty value, where number is a real number greater than 0

arguments.name: DEGREE=
arguments.description: specifies the degree that is used in a polynomial kernel

arguments.name: EPSILON=
arguments.description: specifies the insensitive loss value, where number is a nonnegative real number

arguments.name: KERNEL=
arguments.description: specifies the type of kernel

arguments.name: METHOD=
arguments.description: specifies the optimization method

arguments.name: RBFPARAMETER=
arguments.description: specifies the K_PAR parameter that is used in an RBF kernel, where number is greater than or equal to 0.0001

arguments.name: REGL2=
arguments.description: specifies the L2 penalty value when METHOD=CD, where number is a real number greater than 0

arguments.name: SIGMOIDPARAMETER1=
arguments.description: specifies the K_PAR1 parameter that is used in a sigmoid kernel, where number is a positive number

arguments.name: SIGMOIDPARAMETER2=
arguments.description: specifies the K_PAR2 parameter that is used in a sigmoid kernel, where number is a real number

arguments.name: INTINPUT=
arguments.description: specifies the interval input variables for the base learner model

arguments.name: NOMINPUT=
arguments.description: specifies the nominal input variables for the base learner model

arguments.name: TREESPLIT
arguments.description: specifies a tree-based statistical model

arguments.name: CRITERION=
arguments.description: specifies the criterion by which to split a parent node into child nodes

arguments.name: DEGREE=
arguments.description: specifies the maximum depth of the tree to be grown

arguments.name: MINLEAFSIZE=
arguments.description: specifies the minimum number of observations per child node

arguments.name: NUMBIN=
arguments.description: specifies the number of bins to use for binning interval predictor variables

arguments.name: INTINPUT=
arguments.description: specifies the interval input variables for the base learner model

arguments.name: NOMINPUT=
arguments.description: specifies the nominal input variables for the base learner model

statements.name: CROSSVALIDATION
statements.description: controls details about the k-fold cross-validation process

arguments.name: KFOLD=
arguments.description: specifies the number of partition folds in cross-validation, where n is an integer between 2 and the number of observations that are used for training, inclusive

arguments.name: STRATVAR=
arguments.description: specifies a categorical variable for stratified cross-validation

statements.name: INPUT
statements.description: specifies one or more predictor variables for the super learner model that share a common variable type

arguments.name: LEVEL=
arguments.description: specifies the variable type

statements.name: MARGIN
statements.description: computes a predictive margin. You specify a unique name in single quotes, followed by one or more variable-value pairs that define the predictive margin.

statements.name: OUTPUT
statements.description: creates a data table that contains observationwise predicted values that PROC SUPERLEARNER computes after fitting the model

arguments.name: OUT=
arguments.description: names the output data table for PROC SUPERLEARNER to use

arguments.name: COPYVAR=
arguments.description: transfers one or more variables from the input data table to the output data table

arguments.name: LEARNERPRED
arguments.description: computes predicted response values by using each trained base learner model

arguments.name: MARGINPRED
arguments.description: outputs the predicted response values under each intervening scenario that you specify in a MARGIN statement

statements.name: STORE
statements.description: saves the context and results of the statistical analysis

statements.name: TARGET
statements.description: specifies the response variable for the super learner model

arguments.name: LEVEL=
arguments.description: specifies the variable type
